rationale: Title X is clearer and more complete because it states the comparison, device population, study type, and both major outcomes: bleeding and mortality. Title Y adds the sample size, which improves specificity, but its wording is less smooth and it narrows the apparent focus to bleeding risk while omitting mortality.
loser_weakness: Title Y has awkward syntax and underrepresents the outcome scope by emphasizing bleeding risk without clearly signaling mortality outcomes.
